frameworks.name: ambiguity aversion
frameworks.one_line: preference distinguishing known-probability risk from unknown-probability ambiguity

frameworks.name: incidental affect
frameworks.one_line: affect from a task-irrelevant stimulus biases concurrent uncertainty choices

frameworks.name: computational choice model
frameworks.one_line: parametric model fit to lottery choices to estimate ambiguity attitudes

core_question: Does inducing incidental negative affect during ambiguous lotteries shift ambiguity preferences?
key_assumptions: risk and ambiguity are distinct uncertainty types with separable attitudes
an emotionally evocative image paired with a lottery induces incidental affect
ambiguity attitudes can be recovered from raw choices and a computational model
manipulations: uncertainty type per trial: risky vs ambiguous lottery
pairing of ambiguous lotteries with neutral vs emotionally evocative images
winning probability and amount across lotteries
key_findings: participants were more averse to ambiguity when paired with evocative images
effect appears in both raw choice data and the computational model
interpretations: changing one's affective response can change choices under ambiguity
incidental affect is a causal lever on ambiguity aversion, not just risk
